title: Atitara
taxonomy: Atitara is classified as a genus of flowering plants in the family Euphorbiaceae. The genus Atitara contains a single species, Atitara stenophylla. Atitara stenophylla is native to Mexico and is often found in dry, rocky areas. Atitara stenophylla is a shrub or small tree that produces small white flowers. The classification of Atitara within the plant kingdom is still a subject of ongoing research and study.

The plant's extracts have been reported to possess anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. In some cultures, Atitara is used to treat wounds and reduce pain. Its leaves and stems are often used in herbal remedies to promote healing and prevent infection.
